name: Fresh Strawberry Pie Glace
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the strawberries and rinse them in cold water, then drain and hull them. Reserve some for garnish.
2. Crush 1 pint of the berries with a potato masher or blender. Measure the crushed berries and add enough water to make 1½ cups. Bring the mixture to a boil over low heat, stirring constantly. The mixture will thicken and become translucent. Strain it, then stir in butter (or margarine), lemon juice, and food coloring until cool.
3. Meanwhile, toss the remaining 2 pints of berries with Cointreau or orange juice. Let stand about 30 minutes.
4. Arrange the berries in the pie shell and pour over the cooled glaze.
5. Refrigerate until well chilled, about 2 hours.
6. Before serving, whip cream until stiff and fold in confectioners' sugar.
7. Garnish the pie with whipped cream and reserved strawberries.
NOTE: A nice variation is to combine 1 3 oz package of cream cheese, softened and mixed with 2 tablespoons sour cream, and spread it over the bottom of the pie crust before filling the shell. Alternatively, try a graham cracker crust. This is the same recipe that Aunt Betty and I got from Better Homes and Gardens when we were first married. She still makes it all the time. Remember this one kids?
